subject: Public Health
title: Cultural Perspectives on Mental Health
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Cultural Perspectives on Mental Health.

hook.text: In some cultures, depression is expressed as stomach pain. In others, it's described as a heavy heart.

buildup.text: Mental health concepts don't translate perfectly—Western diagnostic categories may not fit every culture.

discovery.text: Somatization—experiencing psychological distress as physical symptoms—is common in many Asian and African cultures.

twist.text: Culturally adapted therapies work better than imported Western models—context shapes healing.

climax.text: Global mental health must balance universal science with cultural humility and local knowledge.

punchline.text: Healing must speak the patient's language—literally and culturally.

quiz.question: What is somatization?
options: Experiencing psychological distress as physical symptoms
Diagnosing mental illness through blood tests
